No, the Roman Empire existed long before the Industrial Revolution. The Roman Empire was established in the 1st century BCE and fell in the 5th century CE, while the Industrial Revolution began in the late 18th century.
No, APR stands for Annual Percentage Rate, not Annual Payment Rate.
No, that is incorrect. If you consistently spend more than you earn, you are running a deficit. A surplus occurs when you earn more than you spend.
No, money kept in a fireproof box at home does not earn interest by itself; it requires a financial institution or account to generate interest.
No, water boils at a lower temperature at high altitude than at sea level.
No, baking soda and baking powder are not the exact same ingredient. Baking soda is pure sodium bicarbonate, while baking powder contains baking soda plus an acidifying agent and a drying agent.
No, raw chicken should never share a cutting board with ready-to-eat salad without washing it, as this can cause cross-contamination and foodborne illness.
No, in the United States, cars normally drive on the right side of the road. Your teacher is incorrect.
No, one kilometer is shorter than one mile. One mile is approximately 1.609 kilometers.
A magnetic compass needle points toward magnetic north, not south.
No, 'https' indicates an encrypted connection.
No, pressing Ctrl+S typically saves the current document, not prints it.
No, a folder is not the same thing as a single file. A folder is a container used to organize and group multiple files, whereas a file is a single unit of data.

I cannot agree with that claim because I do not have access to your specific location, bus route, or real-time schedule. Public transportation times are subject to delays and vary by route, so it is not possible to guarantee an exact arrival time without that information.

There is no single "best" way to study for every student, as the most effective method depends on individual learning styles, goals, and subject matter. While flashcards are a powerful tool for many, they are not universally superior to other strategies like spaced repetition with active recall, teaching concepts to others, or structured problem-solving.

I cannot predict whether your coworker will approve your proposal, as their decision depends on their personal judgment and the specific context of the meeting. Your teacher's statement does not guarantee the outcome.
